rationale: Although Zhang et al. (2010) is briefly cited in background, it is also discussed explicitly in the theory/limitations section to interpret differences in findings. The authors argue that power distance belief and perceived economic mobility are conceptually distinct, then compare empirical implications across the two studies. This is more than a passing mention because the citation is used to establish construct boundaries and clarify the paper's contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Zhang et al. (2010) as a direct comparison point when distinguishing perceived economic mobility from power distance beliefs in explaining impulsive spending. It contrasts its own pattern (no main effect of low mobility and a materialism interaction) with Zhang et al.'s power-distance effect to position its mechanism as distinct.
